response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack engineer, but the profile is a poor match for this role. C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI) plus an academic thesis project — far short of the 5–10 years of strong, hands-on C++ on Windows required. There is no SQL Server or Oracle experience (only MySQL/MongoDB/generic SQL), no Windows-platform focus (work is largely Linux-based), and no fixed-income, capital-markets, or investment-banking domain background. The primary stack (TypeScript/Node/DLang) does not align with the mandatory requirements.
strengths: Broad, deep software engineering experience across web, mobile, and embedded systems
Strong engineering practices: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development
Excellent communication skills evidenced by workshops, talks, and teaching
concerns: Only ~6 months of C++ experience versus required 5–10 years on Windows
No SQL Server/Oracle and no capital-markets or fixed-income domain knowledge
Core stack (TS/Node/DLang) misaligned with C++/Windows financial-systems role
